trade.summary: Italy and Sweden have strong bilateral trade. Italy exports machinery, vehicles, food, and fashion; Sweden exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, vehicles, and paper products. Both are important EU manufacturing economies with complementary industrial strengths. Volvo trucks and Electrolux have significant Italian market presence.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and Sweden are EU and NATO allies (Sweden joined NATO in 2024). Diplomatic relations are warm and frequent within EU frameworks. Italy and Sweden differ somewhat on fiscal policy (Sweden tends toward fiscal conservatism) and migration. Sweden's new NATO membership has increased defense alignment with Italy.

military.summary: Both are NATO allies following Sweden's accession in March 2024. Italy supported Sweden's NATO application. Saab (Swedish) has some Italian defense market presence. Joint NATO exercises and interoperability. Both operate Gripen and Eurofighter-class aircraft (Italy flies Eurofighter; Sweden has Gripen).

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Swedish multinationals (Ericsson, ABB, Ikea, H&M, Volvo) have major Italian operations. Italian companies have some Swedish market presence. Strong corporate linkages through EU single market.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Some cooperation on energy transition. Sweden's excess renewable electricity and Italy's need for clean energy imports could create future linkages through European grid integration.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation through EU frameworks. Karolinska and Italian medical schools have exchanges. Joint research in life sciences, materials, and climate technology. Both countries are major contributors to EU Horizon research programs.
